name: Lenco
description_short: Lenco Credit Union serves education and government employees in Adrian and surrounding communities with savings, checking, loans, mortgages, and credit cards since 1970.
description_long: Lenco Credit Union was established in 1970 and has grown to serve the banking needs of education and government employees in Adrian and the surrounding communities. As a member-owned financial cooperative, the institution operates with a mission to provide accessible financial services to a specific demographic of working professionals. The credit union maintains physical branch locations and offers modern digital banking capabilities to serve both in-person and remote members. Lenco offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including savings accounts, checking accounts, certificates of deposit, consumer loans, auto loans (new and used), mortgages, and VISA credit cards. Their loan rates are competitively positioned, with new and used auto loans starting at 5.19% APR and VISA credit cards from 7.99% APR. Members can access online banking, bill pay services, a 24/7 login system (It's ME 24/7), and a dedicated mortgage center. The credit union provides financial calculators and maintains a routing number (#272476268) for wire transfers and direct deposits. Lenco distinguishes itself through membership eligibility tied to employment in education and government sectors, creating a specialized member base. The institution emphasizes low-cost and no-cost services, including money orders ($1.00 each), night deposit facilities, payroll deduction programs, direct deposit, and remote check deposit capabilities. Their long operational history since 1970 and community focus in Adrian provides institutional stability and local market knowledge that larger national banks cannot replicate. The primary limitation is membership restriction to education and government employees, which excludes general consumers. The website lacks detailed information about specific membership requirements, fee structures, account minimums, or comprehensive rate comparisons. While digital services are available, the institution appears primarily branch-dependent with limited geographic presence. Prospective members must verify employment eligibility before pursuing membership.
services: Regular Share Accounts (savings) with rates up to 0.50%
Checking accounts with online bill pay
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive rates
Mortgage services including purchase and refinance
Money orders up to $1,000 for $1.00 each
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.19% APR for both new and used vehicles
Remote deposit capability allowing members to deposit checks electronically via photo
No-cost services including night deposit, payroll deduction, and direct deposit
Money orders available at only $1.00 each
24/7 online banking access through It's ME 24/7 platform
Dedicated mortgage center with specialized loan programs
Co-Op network locator providing expanded ATM access beyond Lenco locations
Established 50+ year history demonstrating institutional stability and community commitment
cons: Membership restricted to education and government employees, limiting eligibility for general consumers
Limited geographic footprint with only two physical branch locations mentioned (Maple Office and Branch office)
Website lacks transparency on membership requirements, account fees, minimum balances, and specific rate details
No indication of 24/7 customer service phone support or chat availability despite online banking platform
Limited information about business banking products or services for small business owners
best_for: Education sector employees (teachers, administrators, school staff) seeking member-owned banking
Government employees in Adrian area looking for credit union membership and competitive loan rates
Individuals seeking long-term relationship banking with a stable, established local institution
Members prioritizing low-cost services like remote deposit and direct deposit convenience
diagnosis: Lenco Credit Union is best for education and government employees in the Adrian area who qualify for membership and seek competitive consumer banking products with a personal touch from an established local institution. The main caveat is strict membership eligibility—only education and government employees can join, making it inaccessible to general consumers regardless of financial need or creditworthiness.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Lenco Credit Union serves education and government employees in Adrian with savings, checking, loans, mortgages, and credit cards since 1970.
